context: Tests whether the agent produces structured acceptance criteria for an iOS Safari scroll-snap bug fix without writing code or proposing a solution. Covers bug analysis, root cause investigation, reproduction steps, cross-browser scope, and regression prevention.

checklist.name: Bug clearly restated
checklist.description: Accurately summarizes the reported behavior (carousel snaps back to first item), the affected browser (iOS Safari only), and the trigger condition (swipe to next testimonial)

checklist.name: Root cause analysis
checklist.description: Identifies the CSS change from overflow-x: auto to overflow-x: scroll as the likely cause; discusses the scroll-snap and overflow interaction on iOS Safari

checklist.name: Expected behavior defined
checklist.description: Clearly states what correct scrolling behavior looks like: carousel advances to the next item on swipe and stays there, consistently across all browsers

checklist.name: Reproduction criteria
checklist.description: Describes how to reproduce the bug: iOS Safari, swipe gesture on the carousel, and any specific conditions (e.g., number of items, scroll speed)

checklist.name: Edge cases identified
checklist.description: Addresses edge cases such as rapid swiping, swiping past the last item, single-item carousel, orientation change during scroll, and programmatic scroll

checklist.name: Cross-browser scope
checklist.description: Acceptance criteria ensure the fix does not break behavior on Chrome, Firefox, or desktop Safari

checklist.name: No premature solution
checklist.description: Analyzes the problem without prescribing a specific CSS fix or implementation approach

checklist.name: Responsive behavior considered
checklist.description: Mentions different viewport behaviors if relevant (e.g., touch vs non-touch, portrait vs landscape)

checklist.name: Definition of done is testable
checklist.description: Includes a specific browser/device test matrix (e.g., iOS Safari 16+, Chrome latest, Firefox latest, desktop Safari)

checklist.name: Regression prevention
checklist.description: Mentions the need for a test or verification step to prevent re-introduction of the bug in future refactors
